terminology.throttle_gpio_1_active_time_nc0_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_1
terminology.throttle_activity_2_active_time_nc1_perc: Percent of total time spent being throttled by activity_2
terminology.hardware_dynamic_dma_packet_count: Number of DMA packets that are dynamically generated during execution via dedicated hardware.
terminology.dma_transfer_time: Total duration of all DMA transfers. A DMA transfer is a high level compiler construct representing the movement of one or more tensors, while DMA packets are standard DMA terminology and are the smallest unit of DMA data movement displayed in the Neuron Profiler timeline. A single DMA transfer is fulfilled by multiple DMA packets. The Neuron Profile timeline displays DMA Packets when "Show expanded DMA" is ON, otherwise it aggregates them into DMA transfers.
terminology.sbuf_write_bytes: Total size of all writes to the State Buffer. This includes DMAs writing to and instructions with output to the State Buffer.
terminology.dma_transfer_average_bytes: Total transfer size of all DMA packets divided by the total number of DMA transfers. A DMA transfer is a high level compiler construct representing the movement of one or more tensors, while DMA packets are standard DMA terminology and are the smallest unit of DMA data movement displayed in the Neuron Profiler timeline. A single DMA transfer is fulfilled by multiple DMA packets. The Neuron Profile timeline displays DMA Packets when "Show expanded DMA" is ON, otherwise it aggregates them into DMA transfers.
terminology.output_queue_bytes: Total transfer size of DMA blocks in "output" queues. The "output" queues are primarily for writing output data from the State Buffer to HBM.
terminology.throttle_gpio_5_active_time_nc0_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_5
terminology.throttle_gpio_3_avg_util_limit_nc0_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_3
terminology.hbm_write_bytes: Total bytes of data written to HBM using the DMA engines. This does not include data movement from host DRAM to HBM because this happens before the execution profiling session begins.
terminology.ultraserver_size: The total numbers of instance in an UltraServer.
terminology.cc_cores_instruction_active_time_percent: Percentage of time when at least one Collective Communication (CC) core is processing an instruction (excluding semaphore waits).
terminology.throttle_gpio_5_avg_util_limit_nc0_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_5
terminology.cc_cores_instruction_count: Number of instructions on all Collective Communication (CC) cores.
terminology.throttle_periodic_avg_util_limit_nc1_per: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by periodic
terminology.gpsimd_engine_active_time: Duration of time when GpSimd engine is processing at least one instruction (excluding semaphore waits). Units are in seconds.
terminology.hardware_dynamic_dma_size_percent: Percentage of total transfer size for DMA packets that are dynamically generated via dedicated hardware.
terminology.throttle_active_nc1_time_ns: Total time in nsec when hardware utilization was limited to less than 100% due to throttling.
terminology.throttle_sw_1_avg_util_limit_nc1_percent: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by sw_1
terminology.throttle_gpio_6_active_time_nc0_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_6
terminology.instance_type: The instance type on which this profile was captured.
terminology.throttle_active_nc0_time_ns: Total time in nsec when hardware utilization was limited to less than 100% due to throttling.
terminology.throttle_activity_3_avg_util_limit_nc0_p: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by activity_3
terminology.throttle_gpio_0_avg_util_limit_nc0_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_0
terminology.dma_active_time_percent: Percentage of time when at least one DMA packet is transfering.
terminology.mfu_hlo_max_achievable_estimated_percent: The best Tensor Engine Utilization you can achieve for this workload based on HLO model FLOPs. Calculated as (model_flops / (hbm_write_bytes + hbm_read_bytes)) / (max_ops_per_sec / max_hbm_bandwidth). If mfu_hlo_estimated_percent is much smaller than this value, then this likely indicates a major data movement inefficiency introduced by compilation or kernel implementation for NKI. If mfu_hlo_estimated_percent is close to this value and the mfu_hlo_estimated_percent value is also very high, then there's no point reducing HBM traffic. But if the values are close and mfu_hlo_estimated_percent is lower than you want, consider improving the arithmetic intensity of your workload by performing more computation on every data access. Note: this will be 0 if HLO stats are missing, which is the case for NKI kernels.
terminology.qgpsimdioparam*: DMA queue where DMAs are triggered by GpSimd engine. Used for transferring weights.
terminology.model_flops: Model FLOPs is the FLOP count of useful compute on the Tensor Engine i.e. the matrix multiplications from the HLO model definition, and excludes other instructions for data movement (i.e. transposes and partition broadcasts) inserted by the compiler to resolve memory layout conflicts. Note, each floating point multiply-add is counted as two FLOPs. Calculated as 2 * MAC_count from the HLO stats. Note: this will be 0 if HLO stats are missing, which is the case for NKI kernels.
terminology.throttle_gpio_4_active_time_nc0_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_4
terminology.throttle_sw_1_active_time_nc1_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by sw_1
terminology.throttle_activity_0_avg_util_limit_nc0_p: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by activity_0
terminology.tensor_engine_active_time: Duration of time when Tensor engine is processing at least one instruction (excluding semaphore waits). Units are in seconds.
terminology.throttle_gpio_6_avg_util_limit_nc0_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_6
terminology.throttle_gpio_3_active_time_nc1_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_3
terminology.cc_op_active_time: Duration of time when at least one Collective Communication (CC) operation is active. Units are in seconds.
terminology.static_dma_active_time: Duration of time when at least one statically generated DMA packet is transfering.
terminology.mm_arithmetic_intensity: The ratio of regular MATMUL flops to total DRAM transfer size. If peak_flops_bandwidth_ratio is greater than this value, the workload is memory bound. If it is less than this value, the workload is compute bound. It is calculated as (hardware_flops - transpose_flops) / (hbm_write_bytes + hbm_read_bytes).
terminology.throttle_sw_0_active_time_nc1_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by sw_0
terminology.qgpsimdspillreload*: DMA queue where DMAs are triggered by GpSimd engine. Used for State Buffer spilling and reloading.
terminology.dma_queue_count: Total number of DMA queues specified in the NEFF including all input, output, and weight DMA queues for different DMA engines.
terminology.throttle_gpio_2_active_time_nc1_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_2
terminology.hardware_dynamic_dma_active_time: Duration of time when at least one hardware generated DMA packet is transfering.
terminology.throttle_avg_util_limit_nc1_percent: Average of the hardware utilization limit over the entire execution
terminology.mfu_max_achievable_estimated_percent: The best Tensor Engine Utilization you can achieve for this workload. When HLO stats are available, this is calculated as (model_flops / (hbm_write_bytes + hbm_read_bytes)) / (max_ops_per_sec / max_hbm_bandwidth). When HLO stats are not available (e.g., for NKI kernels), this is calculated as ((hardware_flops - transpose_flops) / (hbm_write_bytes + hbm_read_bytes)) / (max_ops_per_sec / max_hbm_bandwidth). If mfu_estimated_percent is much smaller than this value, it likely indicates a major data movement inefficiency. If mfu_estimated_percent is close to this value and also very high, then there's no point reducing HBM traffic. But if the values are close and mfu_estimated_percent is lower than desired, consider improving the arithmetic intensity of your workload by performing more computation on every data access.
terminology.activate_instruction_time: Total duration of all ACTIVATE/ACTIVATE_QUANTIZE instructions. Overlapping instructions are added up. Units are in seconds.
terminology.spill_save_bytes: Total bytes of spilled data that was saved to HBM. Spilled data is the intermediate tensors computed by the engines that cannot fit in the SBUF during execution and must be spilled into HBM.
terminology.throttle_gpio_1_active_time_nc1_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_1
terminology.throttle_sw_1_avg_util_limit_nc0_percent: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by sw_1
terminology.collectives_version: The Neuron Collectives library version with which this profile was captured.
terminology.runtime_version: The Neuron Runtime library version with which this profile was captured.
terminology.throttle_gpio_5_active_time_nc1_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_5
terminology.throttle_gpio_7_avg_util_limit_nc0_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_7
terminology.dma_transfer_count: Number of DMA transfers. A DMA transfer is a high level compiler construct representing the movement of one or more tensors, while DMA packets are standard DMA terminology and are the smallest unit of DMA data movement displayed in the Neuron Profiler timeline. A single DMA transfer is fulfilled by multiple DMA packets. The Neuron Profile timeline displays DMA Packets when "Show expanded DMA" is ON, otherwise it aggregates them into DMA transfers.
terminology.qsyncioparam*: DMA queue where DMAs are triggered by Sync engine. Used for transferring weights.
terminology.ultraserver_id: Unique UltraServer ID if this profile was captured on an UltraServer instance.
terminology.throttle_gpio_0_active_time_nc0_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_0
terminology.throttle_activity_0_avg_util_limit_nc1_p: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by activity_0
terminology.static_dma_active_time_percent: Percentage of time when at least one statically generated DMA packet is transfering.
terminology.software_dynamic_dma_active_time_percent: Percentage of time when at least one software generated DMA packet is transfering.
terminology.software_dynamic_dma_size_percent: Percentage of total transfer size for DMA packets that are dynamically generated via software backend.
terminology.static_dma_packet_count: Number of DMA packets that are statically generated.
terminology.qsyncspillreload*: DMA queue where DMAs are triggered by Sync engine. Used for State Buffer spilling and reloading.
terminology.throttle_gpio_7_active_time_nc1_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_7
terminology.throttle_gpio_2_avg_util_limit_nc0_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_2
terminology.hardware_dynamic_dma_packet_percent: Percentage of DMA packets that are dynamically generated during execution via dedicated hardware.
terminology.compiler_version: The Neuron Compiler version which built the NEFF that was executed during this profile. This is the tool_version field in the NEFF's info.json file.
terminology.qvectorpio*: DMA queue where DMAs are triggered by Vector engine. Used for transferring inputs/outputs.
terminology.neuroncore_cycle_count: Number of NeuronCore cycles over the duration of the profile i.e. the duration in seconds multiplied by the NeuronCore base clock rate The NeuronCore base clock rate for Inferentia1 is 1.0 GHz, Trainium1 is 1.4 GHz, and Trainium2 is 1.2 GHz.
terminology.mfu_inst_max_achievable_estimated_percen: The best Tensor Engine Utilization you can achieve for this workload based on instruction trace FLOPs (excluding transposes). Calculated as ((hardware_flops - transpose_flops) / (hbm_write_bytes + hbm_read_bytes)) / (max_ops_per_sec / max_hbm_bandwidth). If mfu_inst_estimated_percent is much smaller than this value, then this likely indicates a major data movement inefficiency. If mfu_inst_estimated_percent is close to this value and the mfu_inst_estimated_percent value is also very high, then there's no point reducing HBM traffic. But if the values are close and mfu_inst_estimated_percent is lower than you want, consider improving the arithmetic intensity of your workload by performing more computation on every data access.
terminology.throttle_gpio_3_avg_util_limit_nc1_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_3
terminology.cc_cores_instruction_time: Total duration of instructions on all Collective Communication (CC) cores. Overlapping instructions on the cores are added together.
terminology.dynamic_dma_packet_percent: Percentage of DMA packets that are dynamically generated during execution (generally higher means better performance).
terminology.hfu_estimated_percent: HFU is Hardware FLOPs Utilization. This reflects the Tensor Engine utilization calculated from all Tensor Engine instructions that Neuron Compiler emits for execution. This metric is typically higher than mfu_hlo_estimated_percent because it includes matmul instructions for data movement (i.e. transposes and partition broadcasts) inserted by the compiler to resolve memory layout conflicts. Note, each floating point multiply-add is counted as two FLOPs. Calculated as hardware_flops / (tensor_engine_max_ops_per_sec * total_time) where tensor_engine_max_ops_per_sec is 2 times the number of Tensor Engine elements times the clock speed.
terminology.throttle_sw_1_active_time_nc0_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by sw_1
terminology.software_dynamic_dma_active_time: Duration of time when at least one software generated DMA packet is transfering.
terminology.ultraserver_instance_index: The instance index within the UltraServer on which this profile was captured.
terminology.static_dma_packet_percent: Percentage of DMA packets that are statically generated.
terminology.qscalarpio*: DMA queue where DMAs are triggered by Scalar engine. Used for transferring inputs/outputs.
terminology.sbuf_read_bytes: Total size of all reads from the State Buffer. This includes DMAs reading from and instructions with input from the State Buffer.
terminology.throttle_periodic_active_time_nc1_percen: Percent of total time spent being throttled by periodic
terminology.throttle_sw_0_active_time_nc0_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by sw_0
terminology.transpose_flops: 2x the number of MATMUL operations from transposes. A MATMUL operation is counted as a transpose if the instruction type in its debug info is "TRANSyncOSE". This is a subset of hardware_flops.
terminology.static_dma_size: Total transfer size of DMA packets that are statically generated.
terminology.mbu_estimated_percent: Memory Bandwidth Utilization (MBU) is the achieved HBM bandwidth utilization. It is estimated as a ratio of achieved_hbm_bandwidth over hbm_max_bandwidth, where achieved_hbm_bandwidth is total bytes read/written from/to HBM divided by total_time and hbm_max_bandwidth. Calculated as: (hbm_write_bytes + hbm_read_bytes) / total_time / hbm_max_bandwidth. The hbm_max_bandwidth is the maximum HBM bandwidth of a HBM stack. For LNC1 and LNC2, the hbm_max_bandwidth is the same.
terminology.throttle_gpio_1_avg_util_limit_nc1_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_1
terminology.dma_packet_time: Total duration of all DMA engine packets. Overlapping DMA packets across all DMA engines are added together.
terminology.peak_flops_bandwidth_ratio: The ratio of theoretical max Tensor Engine FLOPS to peak DRAM bandwidth. If mm_arithmetic_intensity is less than this value, the workload is memory bound. If it is greater than this value, the workload is compute bound.
terminology.throttle_activity_3_avg_util_limit_nc1_p: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by activity_3
terminology.throttle_gpio_4_avg_util_limit_nc1_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_4
terminology.throttle_activity_1_avg_util_limit_nc0_p: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by activity_1
terminology.throttle_activity_2_active_time_nc0_perc: Percent of total time spent being throttled by activity_2
terminology.throttle_periodic_avg_util_limit_nc0_per: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by periodic
terminology.static_dma_size_percent: Percentage of total transfer size for DMA packets that are statically generated.
terminology.profiler_version: The Neuron Profile version which post-processed this captured profile.
terminology.software_dynamic_dma_size: Total transfer size of DMA packets that are dynamically generated during execution via software backend.
terminology.input_queue_bytes: Total transfer size of DMA blocks in "input" queues. The "input" queues are primarily for loading input data from HBM to the State Buffer.
terminology.throttle_gpio_6_avg_util_limit_nc1_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_6
terminology.throttle_avg_util_limit_nc0_percent: Average of the hardware utilization limit over the entire execution
terminology.sync_engine_active_time: Duration of time when Sync engine is processing at least one instruction (excluding semaphore waits). Units are in seconds.
terminology.tensor_engine_active_time_percent: Percentage of time when Tensor engine is processing at least one instruction (excluding semaphore waits).
terminology.throttle_sw_0_avg_util_limit_nc0_percent: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by sw_0
terminology.throttle_gpio_0_active_time_nc1_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_0
terminology.inputs_outputs_weights_size_bytes: Total size of input tensors, output tensors, and weight tensors. This is the total necessary HBM reads and writes excluding intermediate tensors, spills and reloads.
terminology.dynamic_dma_active_time_percent: Percentage of time when at least one dynamically generated DMA packet is transfering.
terminology.vector_engine_active_time: Duration of time when Vector engine is processing at least one instruction (excluding semaphore waits). Units are in seconds.
terminology.cc_op_time: Total duration of all Collective Communication (CC) operations. Overlapping operations are added together. Units are in seconds.
terminology.vector_engine_active_time_percent: Percentage of time when Vector engine is processing at least one instruction (excluding semaphore waits).
terminology.hardware_dynamic_dma_active_time_percent: Percentage of time when at least one hardware generated DMA packet is transfering.
terminology.throttle_gpio_7_active_time_nc0_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_7
terminology.hardware_flops: Hardware FLOPs is the FLOP count calculated from all Tensor Engine instructions that Neuron Compiler emits for execution. This metric is typically higher than model_flops because it includes matmul instructions for data movement (i.e. transposes and partition broadcasts) inserted by the compiler to resolve memory layout conflicts. Note, each floating point multiply-add is counted as two FLOPs. Calculated as the sum of (2 * tensor_matrix_active_rows * tensor_matrix_active_cols * elements) for each matrix multiplication.
terminology.throttle_gpio_7_avg_util_limit_nc1_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_7
terminology.hardware_dynamic_dma_size: Total transfer size of DMA packets that are dynamically generated during execution via dedicated hardware.
terminology.total_time: Total duration of on device time for the run. This doesn't include any host<->device data movement overhead or host runtime/framework overhead. Units are in seconds.
terminology.weight_size_bytes: Total size of all weights defined in the NEFF.
terminology.scalar_engine_active_time: Duration of time when Scalar engine is processing at least one instruction (excluding semaphore waits). Units are in seconds.
terminology.tensor_engine_instruction_time: Total duration of all Tensor engine instructions. Overlapping instructions on the engine are added together. Units are in seconds.
terminology.throttle_gpio_5_avg_util_limit_nc1_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_5
terminology.vector_engine_instruction_time: Total duration of all Vector engine instructions. Overlapping instructions on the engine are added together. Units are in seconds.
terminology.psum_read_sbuf_write_bytes: Total bytes of data moved from PSUM to SBUF by compute engine instructions.
terminology.event_count: Total number of event notifications, such as for semaphore updates. This count is less than trace_count because it does not count instructions.
terminology.qscalarspillreload*: DMA queue where DMAs are triggered by Scalar engine. Used for State Buffer spilling and reloading.
terminology.dma_transfer_total_bytes: Total transfer size of all DMA transfers. A DMA transfer is a high level compiler construct representing the movement of one or more tensors.
terminology.throttle_sw_0_avg_util_limit_nc1_percent: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by sw_0
terminology.qsyncpio0*: DMA queue where DMAs are triggered by Sync engine. Used for transferring inputs/outputs.
terminology.throttle_activity_1_active_time_nc1_perc: Percent of total time spent being throttled by activity_1
terminology.sync_engine_active_time_percent: Percentage of time when Sync engine is processing at least one instruction (excluding semaphore waits).
terminology.gpsimd_engine_instruction_time: Total duration of all GpSimd engine instructions. Overlapping instructions on the engine are added together. Units are in seconds.
terminology.trace_count: Total number of trace events in the NTFF including instructions, events/semaphores, and DMAs.
terminology.throttle_activity_2_avg_util_limit_nc0_p: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by activity_2
terminology.total_active_time_percent: Percentage of total execution time when at least one engine, DMA, or CC operation is active.
terminology.cc_op_active_time_percent: Percentage of time when at least one Collective Communication (CC) operation is active.
terminology.software_dynamic_dma_packet_count: Number of DMA packets that are dynamically generated during execution via software backend.
terminology.sync_engine_instruction_time: Total duration of all Sync engine instructions. Overlapping instructions on the engine are added together. Units are in seconds.
terminology.qgpsimdpio*: DMA queue where DMAs are triggered by GpSimd engine. Used for transferring inputs/outputs.
terminology.dma_active_time: Duration of time when at least one DMA packet is transfering.
terminology.throttle_periodic_active_time_nc0_percen: Percent of total time spent being throttled by periodic
terminology.driver_version: The Neuron driver version with which this profile was captured.
terminology.spill_reload_bytes: Total bytes of spilled data that was reloaded back to SBUF. Spilled data is the intermediate tensors computed by the engines that cannot fit in the SBUF during execution and must be spilled into HBM. If a spilled tensor is reloaded multiple times into SBUF, this metric will include the spilled tensor size multiplied by the reload count.
terminology.throttle_gpio_0_avg_util_limit_nc1_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_0
terminology.throttle_activity_3_active_time_nc1_perc: Percent of total time spent being throttled by activity_3
terminology.weight_queue_bytes: Total transfer size of DMA blocks in "data" queues. The "data" queues are primarily for loading weights from HBM to the State Buffer. This size may be larger than weight_size_bytes if weights are loaded multiple times or spilled and reloaded.
terminology.throttle_activity_0_active_time_nc1_perc: Percent of total time spent being throttled by activity_0
terminology.psum_read_sbuf_write_count: Number of compute engine instructions that moved data from PSUM to SBUF.
terminology.scalar_engine_instruction_time: Total duration of all Scalar engine instructions. Overlapping instructions on the engine are added together. Units are in seconds.
terminology.throttle_activity_0_active_time_nc0_perc: Percent of total time spent being throttled by activity_0
terminology.s3_bucket_path: Full S3 path where profile's files (NEFF/NTFF/source/debug) are stored and uploaded
terminology.qvectorioparam*: DMA queue where DMAs are triggered by Vector engine. Used for transferring weights.
terminology.throttle_activity_2_avg_util_limit_nc1_p: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by activity_2
terminology.dynamic_dma_size_percent: Percentage of total transfer size for DMA packets that are dynamically generated during execution.
terminology.throttle_activity_3_active_time_nc0_perc: Percent of total time spent being throttled by activity_3
terminology.total_active_time: Total duration of time when at least one engine, DMA, or CC operation is active. Computed as the union of all active intervals. Units are in seconds.
terminology.throttle_gpio_6_active_time_nc1_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_6
terminology.psum_read_bytes: Total bytes of data that are read from PSUM by compute engine instructions.
terminology.mfu_estimated_percent: Model FLOPs Utilization (MFU) reflects the Tensor Engine utilization for useful compute. When HLO stats are available, this is calculated as model_flops / (tensor_engine_max_ops_per_sec * total_time) where model_flops is 2 * MAC_count from HLO stats. When HLO stats are not available (e.g., for NKI kernels), this is calculated as (hardware_flops - transpose_flops) / (tensor_engine_max_ops_per_sec * total_time) based on the instruction trace. Note, each floating point multiply-add is counted as two FLOPs, and tensor_engine_max_ops_per_sec is 2 times the number of Tensor Engine elements times the clock speed.
terminology.throttle_gpio_1_avg_util_limit_nc0_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_1
terminology.mfu_hlo_estimated_percent: MFU (HLO) is Model FLOPs Utilization based on HLO stats. This reflects the Tensor Engine utilization for useful compute i.e. the matrix multiplications from the HLO model definition, and excludes other instructions for data movement (i.e. transposes and partition broadcasts) inserted by the compiler to resolve memory layout conflicts. Note, each floating point multiply-add is counted as two FLOPs. Calculated as model_flops / (tensor_engine_max_ops_per_sec * total_time) where model_flops is 2 * MAC_count from HLO stats and tensor_engine_max_ops_per_sec is 2 times the number of Tensor Engine elements times the clock speed. Note: this will be 0 if HLO stats are missing, which is the case for NKI kernels.
terminology.psum_write_bytes: Total bytes of data that are written to PSUM by compute engine instructions.
terminology.mbu_min_read_util_percent: Estimated minimum possible Memory Bandwidth Utilization (MBU) utilization assuming inputs and weights are read from HBM a single time (no repeat loads). Calculated as: inputs_and_weights_size_bytes / total_time / hbm_max_bandwidth.
terminology.throttle_gpio_4_active_time_nc1_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_4
terminology.mfu_inst_estimated_percent: MFU (Instruction) is Model FLOPs Utilization based on instruction trace. This reflects the Tensor Engine utilization for useful compute calculated from executed instructions, excluding transposes. Unlike mfu_hlo_estimated_percent which uses HLO model stats, this metric uses (hardware_flops - transpose_flops) from the actual instruction trace. Note, each floating point multiply-add is counted as two FLOPs. Calculated as (hardware_flops - transpose_flops) / (tensor_engine_max_ops_per_sec * total_time) where tensor_engine_max_ops_per_sec is 2 times the number of Tensor Engine elements times the clock speed.
terminology.throttle_gpio_4_avg_util_limit_nc0_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_4
terminology.throttle_activity_1_active_time_nc0_perc: Percent of total time spent being throttled by activity_1
terminology.qscalarioparam*: DMA queue where DMAs are triggered by Scalar engine. Used for transferring weights.
terminology.throttle_gpio_3_active_time_nc0_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_3
terminology.dma_active_cycles: dma_active_time multiplied by NC clock speed.
terminology.scalar_engine_active_time_percent: Percentage of time when Scalar engine is processing at least one instruction (excluding semaphore waits).
terminology.cc_cores_instruction_active_time: Duration of time when at least one Collective Communication (CC) core is processing an instruction (excluding semaphore waits).
terminology.inputs_and_weights_size_bytes: Size of input tensors and weight tensors.
terminology.throttle_activity_1_avg_util_limit_nc1_p: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by activity_1
terminology.software_dynamic_dma_packet_percent: Percentage of DMA packets that are dynamically generated during execution via software backend.
terminology.throttle_gpio_2_active_time_nc0_percent: Percent of total time spent being throttled by gpio_2
terminology.gpsimd_engine_active_time_percent: Percentage of time when GpSimd engine is processing at least one instruction (excluding semaphore waits).
terminology.hbm_read_bytes: Total bytes of data read from HBM using the DMA engines. This does not include data movement from HBM to host DRAM because this happens after the execution profiling session.
terminology.qvectorspillreload*: DMA queue where DMAs are triggered by Vector engine. Used for State Buffer spilling and reloading.
terminology.ultraserver_type: The type of instances connectivity within an UltraServer.
terminology.throttle_gpio_2_avg_util_limit_nc1_perce: Average of the hardware utilization limit as dictated by gpio_2

warnings.message: Missing additional dynamic DMA metadata, reported DMA values may be inaccurate. Try capturing the profile with --enable-dge-notifs or NEURON_RT_ENABLE_DGE_NOTIFICATIONS=1 env flag.
Current NEFF is missing compiler metrics. Please re-compile using latest version of the compiler

Missing HLO stats in the NEFF, `model_flops` and `mfu_hlo_estimated_percent` summary metrics will not be available. Consider the metrics (hardware_flops - transpose_flops) and mfu_inst_estimated_percent instead.
